en.extract:
The sooty-capped babbler (Malacopteron affine) is a member of the family Pellorneidae. It occurs in Malaysia, Indonesia, Brunei, Thailand, and Singapore.
The sooty-capped babbler is also known in Malay as rimba tinjau belukar.  Its main diet is small insects.
It is threatened by habitat loss.
There are two subspecies:

M. a. affine (Blyth, 1842) – Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Banyak Islands, Bangka Islands
M. a. phoeniceum Deignan, 1950 – Borneo


References

The '''sooty-capped babbler''' ('''''Malacopteron affine''''') is a member of the family [[Pellorneidae]]. It occurs in [[Malaysia]], [[Indonesia]], [[Brunei]], [[Thailand]], and [[Singapore]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Babblers, scimitar babblers, ground babblers, Alcippe fulvettas – IOC World Bird List |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/babblers/ |access-date=2023-01-27 |website=www.worldbirdnames.org}}</ref>

The sooty-capped babbler is also known in [[Malay language|Malay]] as ''rimba tinjau belukar''.  Its main diet is small insects.

It is threatened by habitat loss.

There are two [[subspecies]]:<ref name=":0" />

* ''M. a. affine'' (Blyth, 1842) – [[Malay Peninsula]], [[Sumatra]], [[Banyak Islands]], [[Bangka Belitung Islands|Bangka Islands]]
* ''M. a. phoeniceum'' [[Herbert Girton Deignan|Deignan]], 1950 – [[Borneo]]

zh.extract:
纯色树鹛（学名：Malacopteron affine），是画眉科树鹛属的一种，分布于文莱、泰国、印度尼西亚和马来西亚。该物种的保护状况被评为近危。
纯色树鹛的平均体重约为17.1克。栖息地包括种植园、亚热带或热带的沼泽林、亚热带或热带的湿润低地林、亚热带或热带严重退化的前森林和河流、溪流。


参考文献
Malacopteron affine. BirdLife.org.   [2011-06-27]. （原始内容存档于2016-03-05）.
